title: DOCKER Container Port Mapping Validation Fixer

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate Docker container port mappings in a Compose file. The function should take a string representing the Compose file and return `true` if the port mappings are valid and `false` otherwise. Port mappings are valid if each port is unique within a service and all ports are in the range 1-65535.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Compose file in YAML format.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the port mappings are valid.
statement.notes: The function should handle cases where a service does not have any port mappings.

constraints.description: Maximum number of services in the Compose file

examples.explanation: The port mappings are valid because each port is unique within the service and all ports are in the range 1-65535.
The port mappings are invalid because port 80 is used twice within the service.

hints.text: Split the Compose file into lines and iterate over each line to extract the service name and port mappings.

editorial.title: DOCKER Container Port Mapping Validation Fixer Editorial
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to iterate over each service in the Compose file and check if the port mappings are unique within the service.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to store all port mappings in a set and check if the size of the set is equal to the number of port mappings.
editorial.optimalSolution: Create a dictionary to store the port mappings for each service. Then, iterate over each service and check if the port mappings are unique. If a duplicate port mapping is found, return `false`. Otherwise, return `true`.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the function checks for uniqueness of port mappings within each service and checks if all ports are in the range 1-65535.
editorial.dryRun: See the solutions section for a step-by-step example run.
commonMistakes: Not checking for uniqueness of port mappings within each service.
Not checking if all ports are in the range 1-65535.
